RFC.description: Create an engineering request for comments
body: # RFC ${1:RFC-000}: ${2:Title}
<!-- Source of truth: proposed direction, cross-team agreement, and final outcome. -->
<!-- Delete non-applicable sections or items before review. Copy list items as needed. -->
- Author: ${3:Name}
- Owner: ${4:Name}
- Reviewers: ${5:Names or roles}
- Created: ${6:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Target decision date: ${7:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Status: ${8|Draft,Proposed,Accepted,Rejected,Superseded|}
## 1. Summary
- ${9:What is proposed, why it matters, and the expected outcome}
## 2. Context and Problem Statement
- Problem domain: ${10:Domain and affected users, systems, stakeholders, or teams}
- Current state: ${11:How the system works today}
- Problem and evidence: ${12:Limitation supported by data, incidents, or feedback}
- Why now: ${13:Reason a decision is needed now}
<!-- Conditional: retain consultation details when the proposal affects identifiable stakeholders or teams. -->
- Consultation: ${14:Affected stakeholders or teams consulted; feedback and unresolved concerns}
## 3. Goals and Non-Goals
- Goals: ${15:Desired outcomes}
- Non-goals: ${16:Outcomes intentionally not pursued}
## 4. Constraints and Assumptions
- Constraints: ${17:Hard, soft, and deliberately imposed limits}
- Assumptions to validate: ${18:Beliefs the proposal depends on}
<!-- Conditional: retain when the proposal must preserve or introduce explicit invariants. -->
## 5. Invariants
<!-- Options: existing, proposed. -->
- ${19:INV-001} [${20|existing,proposed|}]: ${21:Property that must always remain true}
- Scope and evidence: ${22:Where it applies; how it is preserved and verified}
## 6. Proposal
- Architecture and boundaries: ${23:Proposed structure, responsibilities, and ownership}
- Primary workflow: ${24:End-to-end data and control flow}
- Interfaces and data models: ${25:Contracts that change and compatibility expectations}
## 7. Alternatives Considered
- Alternative: ${26:Alternative approach}
- Evaluation: ${27:Benefits, costs, invariant fit, and reason not selected}
- Impact of doing nothing: ${28:Expected outcome without this change}
<!-- Conditional: retain decisions that require explicit agreement or a durable ADR. -->
## 8. Key Design Decisions
<!-- Options: tentative, accepted. -->
- Status: ${31|tentative,accepted|}
- Rationale and record: ${32:Why; related ADR if the decision needs a durable record}
## 9. Impact
<!-- Conditional: retain only affected dimensions. -->
- Compatibility and migration: ${33:API, data, user, and mixed-version impact}
- Security, privacy, and compliance: ${34:Controls, threats, and obligations}
- Performance and cost: ${35:Expected changes and limits}
- Operations and support: ${36:Observability, ownership, and support impact}
## 10. Risks and Tradeoffs
- Rating rubric: ${37:Definitions for Unassessed, Low, Moderate, and High or policy link}
<!-- Options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High. -->
- Risk: ${38:Risk} [${39|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High|}]
- Mitigation and residual risk: ${40:Mitigation; risk remaining afterward}
- Tradeoff: ${41:Benefit accepted in exchange for a stated cost}
<!-- Conditional: retain when the proposal materially affects delivery, migration, or production exposure. -->
## 11. Rollout and Validation
- Delivery sequence: ${42:Ordered rollout or migration phases; owners and tracking links}
- Rollback: ${43:Triggers and safe reversal steps}
- Technical validation: ${44:Evidence required to proceed through rollout}
- Product outcome measures: ${45:PRD metric IDs or links; do not duplicate their definitions}
## 12. Open Questions
- Question: ${46:Unresolved question}
- Owner and target resolution: ${47:Name; date or milestone}
## 13. Final Decision
- Outcome: ${48|Pending,Accepted,Rejected,Superseded|}
- Rationale: ${49:Why this outcome was selected; material objections and how they were resolved}
<!-- Conditional: retain when dissent or unresolved objections remain material to the decision. -->
- Dissent or unresolved objections: ${50:Position, rationale, owner, and resolution or review trigger}
- Decision makers and date: ${51:Names or roles; YYYY-MM-DD}
- Next actions: ${52:Owners, actions, and tracking links}

PRD.description: Create a concise product requirements document
body: # PRD ${1:PRD-000}: ${2:Title}
<!-- Source of truth: user and business outcomes, requirements, and outcome measurement. -->
<!-- Delete non-applicable sections or items before review. Copy list items as needed. -->
- Product owner: ${3:Name}
- Collaborators: ${4:Names or teams}
- Created: ${5:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Target release: ${6:Date or milestone}
- Status: ${7|Draft,In Review,Approved,In Delivery,Complete|}
## 1. Summary
- ${8:What is being delivered, for whom, and why}
- Hypothesis: ${9:If we deliver X for Y, we expect Z because of this evidence or mechanism}
## 2. Problem and Opportunity
- Users and problem: ${10:Target users; problem from their perspective}
- Evidence: ${11:Research, data, or customer feedback}
- Why now and business objective: ${12:Priority rationale; strategic outcome}
## 3. Outcomes, Metrics, and Guardrails
- Desired outcomes: ${13:User and business outcomes}
<!-- Conditional: retain success metrics and guardrails when outcome measurement is required. -->
- Success metrics: ${14:Metric IDs; definitions, baselines, targets, authoritative evidence sources, and owners}
- Guardrails: ${15:Metrics or user guarantees that must not regress; authoritative evidence sources}
- Non-goals: ${16:Outcomes intentionally not pursued}
- Excluded capabilities: ${17:Delivery scope intentionally excluded}
## 4. Users and User Experience
- Stories and primary journey: ${18:Key user stories and end-to-end interaction}
- Design evidence: ${19:Research, flows, prototypes, or design links}
- Edge cases and accessibility: ${20:Important variations and inclusive design requirements}
## 5. Requirements
<!-- Acceptance criteria or a reference is required for every P0 requirement. -->
<!-- Options: TBD, P0, P1, P2, P3. -->
- ${21:REQ-001} [${22|TBD,P0,P1,P2,P3|}]: ${23:Functional requirement — rationale — acceptance criteria or reference}
<!-- Options: TBD, P0, P1, P2, P3. -->
- ${24:NFR-001} [${25|TBD,P0,P1,P2,P3|}]: ${26:Quality, privacy, legal, accessibility, or operational requirement — measurable threshold}
## 6. Dependencies, Constraints, and Assumptions
- Dependencies: ${27:Teams, systems, vendors, or decisions}
- Constraints: ${28:Time, legal, technical, or commercial limits}
- Assumptions to validate: ${29:Beliefs the delivery plan depends on}
## 7. Risks and Mitigations
- Rating rubric: ${30:Definitions for Unassessed, Low, Moderate, and High or policy link}
<!-- Options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High. -->
- ${31:RISK-001} [${32|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High|}]: ${33:Delivery or outcome risk — mitigation or validation activity — owner — tracking link}
## 8. Delivery and Launch
- Rollout phase: ${34|Pilot,Phased rollout,General availability|}
- Delivery plan: ${35:Milestones, release sequence, owners, and tracking links}
- Launch criteria: ${36:Delivery and release evidence required before exposure increases; product outcomes are evaluated after launch}
- Contingency plan: ${37:Response if delivery or guardrail metrics regress}
<!-- Conditional: retain when launch or outcome evaluation requires new instrumentation. -->
- Instrumentation: ${38:Events, dashboards, queries, reports, or manual procedures required before launch}
## 9. Outcome Evaluation
- Post-launch measurement window: ${39:When outcomes will be evaluated after launch completion}
- Analysis owner and decision rules: ${40:Owner; actions for success, ambiguity, or regression}
## 10. Open Questions
- ${41:Question — why unresolved — owner — target resolution date or milestone}
## 11. Ownership and Approval
- Accountable owner: ${42:Name}
- Approvers: ${43:Names or roles}
- Decision and date: ${44:Decision; YYYY-MM-DD}
# ADR ${1:ADR-000}: ${2:Title}
<!-- Source of truth: the durable record and rationale for one architecture decision. -->
<!-- Keep this concise. Link implementation detail rather than duplicating it. -->
- Status: ${3|Proposed,Accepted,Rejected,Deprecated,Superseded|}
- Created: ${4:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Decision date: ${5:YYYY-MM-DD or pending}
- Decision makers: ${6:Names or roles}
- Affected scope: ${7:Components, systems, or decisions to which this applies}
- Supersedes: ${8:None or ADR IDs}
- Superseded by: ${9:None or ADR ID}
## Context and Problem Statement
- Context: ${10:Forces, constraints, and circumstances surrounding the decision}
- Decision question: ${11:Question this record resolves}
## Decision Drivers
<!-- Options: Unassessed, Must-have, High, Moderate, Low. -->
- ${12:DRV-001} [${13|Unassessed,Must-have,High,Moderate,Low|}]: ${14:Quality attribute, requirement, or constraint}
## Invariants
- ${15:INV-001}: ${16:Property every acceptable option must preserve}
## Considered Options
- Option: ${17:Option}
- Evaluation: ${18:Fit against prioritized drivers and invariants}
- Benefits and costs: ${19:Benefits; drawbacks and operational burden}
## Decision Outcome
- Chosen option: ${20:Selected option}
- Rationale: ${21:Why it best satisfies the drivers and invariants}
## Consequences
- Positive: ${22:Expected positive consequences}
- Negative: ${23:Accepted negative consequences}
- Operational: ${24:Follow-up work, ownership, constraints, and tracking links created}
- Reversibility: ${25|Unassessed,Easy,Costly,Effectively irreversible|} — ${26:Conditions and migration cost}
## Confirmation
- ${27:INV-001 or ADR-000: conformance test, monitor, review, or other evidence — owner — due date or review trigger — implementation or tracking link}
## Links
- Related RFCs, PRDs, technical designs, ADRs, or issues: ${28:Links}

Technical Design.description: Create an implementation-ready technical design document
body: # Technical Design ${1:TD-000}: ${2:Title}
<!-- Source of truth: implementation mechanics and evidence of technical readiness. -->
<!-- Delete non-applicable sections or items before review. Copy list items as needed. -->
- Author: ${3:Name}
- Reviewers: ${4:Names or roles}
- Status: ${5|Draft,In Review,Approved,Implemented|}
- Created: ${6:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Related PRD, RFC, and ADRs: ${7:IDs or links}
## 1. Summary
- ${8:What will change and how the design satisfies approved requirements}
## 2. Scope and Requirements
- Scope: ${9:In scope; out of scope}
- Requirements and completion criteria: ${10:Requirement IDs; observable implementation-complete conditions}
## 3. Design Invariants
- ${11:INV-001}: ${12:Property the implementation must always preserve}
- Source, scope, enforcement, and evidence: ${13:RFC or ADR; where it applies; mechanism; test or monitor}
## 4. Existing System
- ${14:Current architecture, integrations, ownership, and inherited constraints}
## 5. Proposed Design
- Architecture and component boundaries: ${15:Structure, responsibilities, and ownership}
- Data and control flow: ${16:End-to-end behavior}
- Interfaces, contracts, and dependencies: ${17:APIs, events, schemas, libraries, services, version changes, and compatibility}
- State, concurrency, and consistency: ${18:State transitions and guarantees}
## 6. Key Design Decisions and Alternatives
- ${19:DEC-001}: ${20:Decision and chosen approach}
- Alternatives, rationale, and related ADR: ${21:Options rejected; why; ADR ID or why one is unnecessary}
## 7. Detailed Changes
- ${22:Component or module: concrete change; inputs and outputs; dependencies; configuration or flags}
## 8. Data and Migration
<!-- Conditional: remove this section when no persisted data or format changes. -->
- Data model: ${23:Schemas, indexes, formats, ownership, and lifecycle}
- Migration: ${24:Backfill sequence, compatibility window, recovery, retention, and deletion}
## 9. Failure Handling
- Failure modes and degraded behavior: ${25:Boundary failures; what remains available and user-visible}
- Error handling and recovery: ${26:Propagation, retries, timeouts, idempotency, and safe recovery}
## 10. Quality Attributes
<!-- Conditional: retain when security, privacy, or compliance behavior changes; otherwise state why it is unaffected. -->
- Security, privacy, and compliance: ${27:Controls, threat considerations, and obligations}
- Performance and capacity budgets: ${28:Baseline, forecast load, latency, throughput, resource, and cost limits}
- Reliability and availability: ${29:SLOs, error budgets, and consistency or durability targets}
<!-- Conditional: retain when the change affects production operation, support, or observability. -->
## 11. Operations and Observability
- Signals: ${30:Logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, and invariant monitors}
- Response and ownership: ${31:Alerts, runbooks, responsible team, and support model}
## 12. Production Rollout and Rollback
- Rollout and compatibility: ${32:Exposure sequence and mixed-version behavior}
- Release-readiness gate: ${33:Tests, operational evidence, and approvals required before exposure}
- Rollback: ${34:Triggers and safe reversal steps}
## 13. Verification
- Test strategy: ${35:Unit, integration, end-to-end, failure, and non-functional checks}
- Traceability: ${36:Requirement and invariant IDs mapped to tests or operational evidence}
- Manual and operational validation: ${37:Smoke tests, shadowing, or production checks}
## 14. Implementation Plan
- Work breakdown and sequencing: ${38:Ordered implementation units, dependencies, owners, and tracking links}
- Implementation-readiness gate: ${39:Decisions and evidence required before coding begins}
## 15. Risks and Open Questions
- Risk: ${40:Implementation risk — mitigation — residual risk — owner — tracking link}
- Open question: ${41:Question — owner — resolution date — whether it blocks approval}
## 16. Approval
- Approvers: ${42:Names or roles}
- Decision and date: ${43:Decision; YYYY-MM-DD}

Architecture Overview.description: Create a living overview of the current system architecture
body: # Architecture Overview ${1:ARCH-000}: ${2:System or Platform}
<!-- Source of truth: the current architecture and its system-wide boundaries. -->
<!-- Describe the deployed system, not a proposed change. Link detailed contracts and runbooks. -->
- Owner: ${3:Name or team}
- Contributors: ${4:Names or teams}
- Status: ${5|Draft,Current,Under Review,Archived|}
- Last updated: ${6:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Next review: ${7:YYYY-MM-DD or review trigger}
- Related systems and documents: ${8:IDs or links}
## 1. Purpose and Scope
- Purpose and audience: ${9:Why this overview exists and who should use it}
- Scope and exclusions: ${10:Included boundaries; explicitly excluded concerns}
## 2. System Context
- Context: ${11:Users, upstream systems, downstream systems, and external dependencies}
- Context diagram: ${12:Diagram or link — last validated date and supporting evidence}
- Boundaries: ${13:Organizational, trust, tenancy, and regional boundaries}
## 3. Capabilities and Components
- ${14:CAP-001}: ${15:Capability}
- Components and ownership: ${16:Components that provide it; responsible teams}
## 4. Data and Control Flows
- ${17:FLOW-001}: ${18:Trigger, path, transformations, storage, outcome, and validation evidence or date}
- Systems of record and data ownership: ${19:Authoritative sources and owners}
## 5. Interfaces and Dependencies
- Interfaces and contracts: ${20:APIs, events, schemas, protocols, and contract links}
- Critical dependencies: ${21:Dependency purpose, failure assumptions, and fallback behavior}
## 6. Invariants and Constraints
- ${22:INV-001}: ${23:Property the deployed system must always preserve}
- Constraints: ${24:Technical, organizational, regulatory, or historical limits}
## 7. Quality Attributes
- Reliability and service levels: ${25:Failure domains, degraded modes, SLOs, consistency, durability, and recovery objectives}
- Security, privacy, and compliance: ${26:Trust model, data classifications, and key controls}
- Performance and capacity: ${27:Workload profile, budgets, scaling model, and known limits}
## 8. Deployment Topology
- Environments and topology: ${28:Regions, zones, networks, tenancy, and runtime placement}
- Build and deployment model: ${29:Artifact flow, release ownership, and configuration boundaries}
## 9. Decisions and Evolution
- Architecture decisions: ${30:ADR index or links}
- Known limitations and debt: ${31:Current gaps, operational or product impact, owners, and tracking links}
- Expected evolution: ${32:Approved direction and linked RFCs; avoid speculative design}
## 10. Maintenance
- Maintainers: ${33:Teams or roles responsible for keeping this current}
- Review evidence and triggers: ${34:How accuracy is checked; changes that require an update}

Data Contract.description: Create a versioned contract for a data product, dataset, or stream
body: # Data Contract ${1:DC-000}: ${2:Data Product or Interface}
<!-- Source of truth: data semantics, ownership, delivery guarantees, and evolution policy. -->
<!-- Link the authoritative machine-readable schema; do not duplicate it in Markdown. -->
- Contract version: ${3:1.0.0}
- Status: ${4|Draft,Active,Deprecated,Retired|}
- Accountable owner: ${5:Name or team}
- Producer: ${6:System and team}
- Support contact: ${7:Channel or rotation}
- Effective date: ${8:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Related documents: ${9:Schema, lineage, RFC, ADR, and runbook links}
## 1. Purpose and Semantics
- Data product or entity: ${10:Dataset, table, event, stream, or API response}
- Business meaning and use: ${11:Meaning, supported decisions, and prohibited interpretations}
## 2. Consumers and Criticality
- Consumer: ${12:System or team — use case — business or operational criticality}
- Downstream dependencies: ${13:Known transitive impact and critical paths}
## 3. Interface and Schema
- Authoritative schema: ${14:URI and format such as Protobuf, Avro, JSON Schema, or ODCS}
- Delivery interface: ${15:Topic, table, object path, endpoint, or sharing protocol}
- Key semantics: ${16:Identifiers, nullability, units, enums, and domain rules not encoded by the schema}
- Compatibility policy: ${17:Backward, forward, and full compatibility guarantees}
## 4. Temporal and Delivery Semantics
- Time semantics: ${18:Event time, processing time, timezone, lateness, and watermark behavior}
- Delivery semantics: ${19:Cadence, partitioning, ordering, duplication, and delivery guarantee}
## 5. Data Quality and Service Levels
- ${20:DQ-001}: ${21:Quality rule — dimension — scope — threshold}
- Service levels: ${22:Freshness, availability, completeness, and incident-response targets}
- Breach communication: ${23:Detection, notification channel, recipients, and expected response}
## 6. Evolution and Correction
- Versioning and change process: ${24:Allowed changes, compatibility checks, notice period, deprecation, and breaking-change exception or waiver process — approver — expiry — tracking link}
- Correction, replay, and backfill: ${25:How defects are corrected and consumers distinguish revisions}
- Lineage: ${26:Sources, transformations, and authoritative lineage link}
## 7. Security and Governance
- Classification: ${27:Sensitivity, personal data, regulated fields, and residency}
- Access and protection: ${28:Authorization, encryption, masking, and audit requirements}
- Retention and deletion: ${29:Retention periods, legal holds, deletion propagation, and verification}
## 8. Obligations
- Producer obligations: ${30:Validation, publication, compatibility, and incident responsibilities}
- Consumer obligations: ${31:Supported usage, deduplication, access, and upgrade responsibilities}
## 9. Validation and Monitoring
- Contract verification: ${32:Producer checks, consumer compatibility tests, and release gates}
- Operational evidence: ${33:Quality monitors, dashboards, alerts, and owners}
## 10. Approval and Review
- Approval and effective date: ${34:Producer and affected consumer representatives; acknowledgement required for incompatible changes; YYYY-MM-DD}
- Next review or retirement trigger: ${35:Date, schema event, ownership change, or deprecation condition}

Service Profile and Runbook.description: Create an operational profile and executable service runbook
body: # Service Profile and Runbook ${1:RUN-000}: ${2:Service or Platform}
<!-- Source of truth: safe operation, diagnosis, recovery, and escalation for the deployed service. -->
<!-- Keep procedures executable and tested. Never include credentials or secret values. -->
- Owning team: ${3:Team}
- On-call or support contact: ${4:Rotation or channel}
- Status: ${5|Draft,Current,Under Review,Deprecated|}
- Criticality: ${6|TBD,Tier 0,Tier 1,Tier 2,Tier 3|} — ${7:Tier definition or policy link}
- Last validated: ${8:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Next review: ${9:YYYY-MM-DD or review trigger}
- Architecture, dashboards, repositories, and contracts: ${10:Links}
## 1. Service Profile
- Purpose and topology: ${11:Responsibilities, runtime placement, and ownership boundaries}
- Critical consumers and dependencies: ${12:Systems, teams, and failure impact}
## 2. Service Levels and Limits
- SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets: ${13:Definitions, targets, and authoritative dashboards}
- Capacity limits: ${14:Traffic, data volume, concurrency, storage, quotas, and scaling thresholds}
- Data guarantees: ${15:Freshness, ordering, consistency, durability, and recovery expectations}
## 3. Access and Safety
- Prerequisites and access: ${16:Roles, tools, environment checks, and audited access path}
- High-risk action guardrails: ${17:Approvals, backups, dry runs, rate limits, and abort conditions}
## 4. Observability
- Investigation entry points: ${18:Dashboards, logs, traces, health checks, and query links}
- Alerts: ${19:Alert meaning, likely causes, urgency, and first response}
## 5. Initial Diagnosis
- First checks: ${20:Fast checks that establish scope, health, recent changes, and data impact}
- Symptom guide: ${21:Symptom — discriminating evidence — next procedure}
## 6. Operational Procedures
- ${22:PROC-001}: ${23:Trigger, preconditions, expected duration, and required privileges}
- Steps and expected evidence: ${24:Ordered commands or actions; expected result after each; last-tested date and evidence}
- Abort, rollback, and escalation: ${25:Stop conditions, reversal steps, and escalation point}
## 7. Data Operations
- Replay, reconciliation, and backfill: ${26:Safe procedures, checkpoints, throttles, and idempotency}
- Data validation: ${27:Counts, checksums, quality queries, quarantine, and consumer confirmation}
## 8. Backup and Disaster Recovery
- Backup policy and objectives: ${28:Backup scope, schedule, retention, RPO, and RTO}
- Restore and failover: ${29:Procedure, validation, ownership, and last exercise result}
## 9. Deployment and Rollback
- Deployment procedure: ${30:Release path, compatibility checks, and approval gates}
- Verification and rollback: ${31:Post-deploy evidence, rollback triggers, and safe reversal}
## 10. Escalation and Communication
- Severity and escalation: ${32:Severity criteria, responders, specialists, and decision authority}
- Communication: ${33:Stakeholders, channels, update cadence, and status template}
## 11. Validation and Maintenance
- Exercise cadence: ${34:Procedure tests, restore drills, game days, and evidence location}
- Known gaps and review triggers: ${35:Unsafe or untested areas — owner — tracking link; changes requiring an update}

Blameless Postmortem.description: Create a blameless, systems-focused incident review
body: # Postmortem ${1:INC-000}: ${2:Incident Title}
<!-- Reconstruct how system conditions made actions reasonable with the information available at the time. -->
<!-- Do not use a singular root cause, human error, individual blame, hindsight judgment, or actions such as “be more careful.” -->
<!-- Separate observed evidence from hypotheses. Improve the system rather than attempting to fix a person. -->
- Severity: ${3|TBD,SEV-1,SEV-2,SEV-3,SEV-4|} — ${4:Severity policy or rubric link and rationale}
- Status: ${5|Draft,Under Review,Published,Follow-up Complete|}
- Incident date: ${6:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Duration and key timestamps: ${7:Start, detection, mitigation, full recovery, and end with timezone; total duration}
- Affected services or data products: ${8:Names or IDs}
- Facilitator: ${9:Name}
- Contributors: ${10:Responders and subject-matter experts}
- Incident channel, timeline, dashboards, and related changes: ${11:Links}
## 1. Executive Summary
- What happened: ${12:Concise factual description of the incident and mechanism}
- Current status: ${13:Recovery state, remaining exposure, and follow-up status}
## 2. Impact
- User and business impact: ${14:Affected users, capabilities, regions, and business processes}
- Data, privacy, and compliance impact: ${15:Loss, delay, corruption, exposure, or confirmed absence of impact}
- Scope and service-level impact: ${16:Duration, volume, SLO breach, and method used to estimate impact}
## 3. Detection and Response
- Detection: ${17:How and when the incident was detected; signal that first identified it}
- Response and recovery: ${18:How responders established scope, mitigated impact, and restored service}
- Detection opportunities: ${19:Signals that existed but were unavailable, ambiguous, or unactionable}
## 4. Evidence-Based Timeline
- ${20:YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM TZ — observed event or decision — evidence link or confidence level}
## 5. System Behavior and Failure Mechanism
- Expected versus observed behavior: ${21:Intended behavior; what the system actually did}
- Propagation: ${22:How conditions interacted and impact spread across boundaries}
## 6. Contributing Conditions and Decision Context
- ${23:COND-001}: ${24:Technical, organizational, environmental, or process condition — supporting evidence}
- Local context: ${25:Information, constraints, incentives, and tradeoffs that made actions reasonable at the time}
- Latent conditions: ${26:Interfaces, defaults, ownership, workload, tooling, or organizational factors that shaped the event}
## 7. Resilience and Response Conditions
- What helped: ${27:Safeguards, expertise, coordination, or system behavior that reduced impact}
- What made response difficult: ${28:Missing information, unsafe controls, ambiguity, coupling, or coordination cost}
## 8. Recovery and Data Reconciliation
- Mitigation and restoration: ${29:Actions taken, evidence of recovery, and temporary controls still active}
- Data reconciliation: ${30:Replay, correction, validation, consumer notification, and unresolved data risk}
## 9. Learnings
- Surprises: ${31:Behavior or interaction that differed from the shared mental model}
- Assumptions and resilience opportunities: ${32:Invalidated assumptions; where the system can better absorb failure}
## 10. System Improvement Actions
<!-- Prefer actions that prevent, detect, contain, or improve learning. Training alone is rarely sufficient. -->
<!-- Options: prevent, detect, mitigate, learn. -->
- ${33:ACT-001} [${34|prevent,detect,mitigate,learn|}]: ${35:System change — priority — owner — due date — tracking issue}
- Verification and status: ${36:Evidence that will demonstrate improvement; current status}
## 11. Evidence Gaps and Unresolved Questions
- Unknowns and confidence: ${37:What remains uncertain; confidence in important explanations}
- Follow-up investigation: ${38:Experiment, instrumentation, or analysis — owner — target date — tracking issue}
## 12. Follow-up and Sharing
- Action review: ${39:Review date, accountable facilitator, and escalation for overdue actions}
- Shared learning: ${40:Runbooks, alerts, designs, training environments, or teams to update}

Data Migration and Backfill Plan.description: Create an executable plan for a high-risk data migration or backfill
body: # Data Migration and Backfill Plan ${1:MIG-000}: ${2:Title}
<!-- Source of truth: execution, reconciliation, cutover, and recovery for this migration. -->
<!-- Make irreversible boundaries explicit. Commands belong in a reviewed runbook or script. -->
- Owner: ${3:Name or team}
- Reviewers: ${4:Data owners, consumers, operations, and security}
- Status: ${5|Draft,Approved,In Progress,Validating,Complete,Aborted|}
- Planned window: ${6:Start and end time with timezone}
- Source and target: ${7:System, dataset, schema, and version identifiers}
- Related technical design, contracts, and runbooks: ${8:Links}
## 1. Summary and Scope
- Change and rationale: ${9:What moves or transforms and why}
- Scope and exclusions: ${10:Included records, partitions, consumers, and excluded work}
## 2. Inventory and Baseline
- Source baseline: ${11:Ownership, volume, partitions, schema, quality, and authoritative counts}
- Target state: ${12:Desired schema, ownership, placement, quality, and expected counts}
## 3. Guarantees and Constraints
- ${13:INV-001}: ${14:Property that must hold before, during, and after migration}
- Execution constraints: ${15:Downtime, throughput, cost, retention, regulatory, and consumer limits}
## 4. Migration Strategy
- Phases: ${16:Preparation, copy, synchronization, validation, cutover, and cleanup — estimated duration for each}
- Transition mechanism: ${17:Dual write, dual read, change capture, freeze, or version routing}
## 5. Compatibility
- Readers, writers, and versions: ${18:Compatibility matrix and upgrade order across the transition}
## 6. Backfill Execution
- Segmentation and progress: ${19:Partitioning, checkpoints, idempotency keys, and completion tracking}
- Resource safety: ${20:Throttles, quotas, scheduling, restart behavior, pause or resume criteria, and production abort thresholds}
## 7. Reconciliation and Acceptance
- ${21:REC-001}: ${22:Count, checksum, semantic, or quality comparison — scope — threshold}
- Exceptions: ${23:Quarantine, investigation, correction, accepted discrepancy process, owner, and tracking link}
## 8. Cutover
- Preconditions and procedure: ${24:Ordered steps, evidence at each gate, and smoke tests}
- Decision and communication: ${25:Decision owner, go or no-go criteria, consumers, and update cadence}
## 9. Rollback and Irreversibility
- Safe rollback: ${26:Triggers, reversal procedure, retained source state, and validation}
- Point of no return: ${27:Irreversible step, approval required, and forward-recovery plan}
## 10. Operations and Observability
- Signals and response: ${28:Progress, errors, lag, data quality, alerts, dashboards, and runbook}
- Capacity and support: ${29:Expected load, impact controls, staffing, and escalation}
## 11. Security and Governance
- Data protection: ${30:Access, encryption, masking, audit, residency, and temporary-copy controls}
- Lifecycle and lineage: ${31:Retention, deletion, catalog, lineage, and ownership updates}
## 12. Testing and Rehearsal
- Rehearsals: ${32:Representative dry runs, failure injection, restore tests, and measured duration}
- Readiness evidence: ${33:Results required before production execution}
## 13. Execution Plan
- Milestones, owners, and dependencies: ${34:Ordered work, handoffs, and tracking links}
## 14. Approval and Completion
- Execution approval: ${35:Approvers and date}
- Completion evidence: ${36:Reconciliation sign-off, consumer confirmation, cleanup and decommissioning tracking links}

Threat Model.description: Create a scoped threat model with traceable controls and residual risk
body: # Threat Model ${1:TM-000}: ${2:System or Change}
<!-- Source of truth: scoped threats, controls, verification, and accepted residual risk. -->
<!-- Model plausible attacker goals and abuse paths, not only component vulnerabilities. -->
- Owner: ${3:Name or team}
- Reviewers: ${4:Security, privacy, platform, and domain representatives}
- Status: ${5|Draft,Under Review,Accepted,Needs Revision,Archived|}
- Method: ${6|STRIDE,LINDDUN,Attack trees,Custom|}
- Rating rubric: ${7:Definitions for likelihood and impact or policy link}
- Created: ${8:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Next review: ${9:YYYY-MM-DD or review trigger}
- Related architecture, data contracts, RFCs, and ADRs: ${10:Links}
## 1. Summary and Scope
- System or change: ${11:Purpose and security-relevant behavior}
- Scope and exclusions: ${12:Included components, actors, data, environments, and explicit exclusions}
- Assumptions: ${13:Trust, deployment, identity, dependency, and attacker assumptions to validate}
## 2. System Model and Trust Boundaries
- Data-flow model: ${14:Components, stores, flows, protocols, and diagram link}
- Trust boundaries and identities: ${15:Boundary crossings, principals, credentials, and authorization domains}
- External dependencies: ${16:Services, libraries, operators, and supply-chain boundaries}
## 3. Assets and Security Objectives
- ${17:ASSET-001}: ${18:Asset and classification — confidentiality, integrity, availability, or privacy objective}
## 4. Actors and Capabilities
- Actor: ${19:Legitimate or adversarial actor — access, capabilities, motivation, and constraints}
## 5. Attack Surfaces
- Surface: ${20:Entry point, privileged operation, parser, dependency, administrative path, or data flow}
## 6. Threat Scenarios
- ${21:THREAT-001}: ${22:Attacker goal, preconditions, path, affected assets, and trust boundary}
<!-- Likelihood options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High. Impact options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High, Critical. -->
- Likelihood: ${23|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High|}; impact: ${24|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High,Critical|}; rationale: ${25:Evidence and uncertainty}
<!-- Options: Open, Mitigated, Accepted, Transferred. -->
- Disposition: ${26|Open,Mitigated,Accepted,Transferred|}; mapped controls or accepted-risk ID: ${27:IDs and rationale}
## 7. Controls and Residual Risk
<!-- Options: preventive, detective, corrective. -->
- ${28:CONTROL-001} [${29|preventive,detective,corrective|}]: ${30:Mechanism — mapped threat IDs — owner — status — tracking link}
- Verification and residual risk: ${31:Test or evidence; remaining exposure after the control}
## 8. Abuse and Privacy Cases
- Abuse or misuse: ${32:Authorized capability used harmfully, accidentally, or beyond intended scope}
- Privacy threat: ${33:Linkability, identifiability, disclosure, consent, or lifecycle risk}
## 9. Accepted Risks
- ${34:RISK-001}: ${35:Residual risk — status — accountable risk owner — rationale — expiry or review date — tracking link}
## 10. Verification and Monitoring
- Security verification: ${36:Tests, reviews, scans, exercises, and release gates}
- Detection and response: ${37:Telemetry, alerts, runbooks, and incident readiness}
## 11. Review and Approval
- Review triggers: ${38:Architecture, data, identity, dependency, exposure, or threat-intelligence changes}
- Approval and date: ${39:Security and accountable owners; YYYY-MM-DD}

Technology Radar Entry.description: Create an evidence-backed technology radar recommendation
body: # Technology Radar ${1:TR-000}: ${2:Technology}
<!-- Source of truth: organization-level guidance for adopting or avoiding this technology. -->
<!-- Link RFCs and ADRs for individual system choices rather than duplicating them here. -->
- Owner: ${3:Name or group}
- Last updated: ${4:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Next review: ${5:YYYY-MM-DD or evidence trigger}
- Quadrant: ${6|Languages and Frameworks,Platforms,Techniques,Tools|}
- Ring: ${7|Adopt,Trial,Assess,Hold|}
- Previous ring: ${8|New,Adopt,Trial,Assess,Hold|}
- Applicability scope: ${9:Organization, domains, teams, or workload classes}
## 1. Recommendation
- Summary and ring rationale: ${10:Recommended posture and the strongest reasons for it}
## 2. Applicability
- Problems and use cases: ${11:Where the technology provides meaningful advantage}
- Non-goals: ${12:Problems it does not solve and contexts outside this assessment}
## 3. Evidence
- Evaluation evidence: ${13:Benchmarks, prototypes, comparisons, linked RFCs or ADRs, and evidence dates}
- Operational and ecosystem evidence: ${14:Production experience, maturity, support, community, roadmap, source dates, and freshness limitations}
- Security, licensing, and cost: ${15:Supply-chain posture, obligations, pricing, and total ownership cost}
## 4. Assessment
- Architecture and interoperability: ${16:Fit with platform direction, standards, data, and integration model}
- Operability: ${17:Reliability, observability, scalability, upgrade, and disaster-recovery characteristics}
- Organizational fit: ${18:Skills, support model, vendor dependence, and switching cost}
## 5. Usage Guidance
- Recommended uses: ${19:Workloads and conditions for which use is encouraged}
- Prohibited or discouraged uses: ${20:Contexts where use creates unacceptable risk or complexity; exception request path, evidence required, owner, and tracking link}
- Prerequisites and guardrails: ${21:Approved versions, patterns, controls, ownership, and review requirements}
## 6. Adoption and Exit
- Trial or adoption plan: ${22:Scope, owner, support, controlled expansion path, and tracking links}
- Success and stop criteria: ${23:Evidence required to advance, remain, or move to Hold}
- Exit strategy: ${24:Portability, data export, replacement, and migration considerations}
## 7. Risks and Unknowns
- Risk: ${25:Risk — mitigation — accountable owner}
- Evidence gaps: ${26:Unknowns, experiments, and evidence needed before the next review}
## 8. Decision Record
- Change from previous posture: ${27:What changed and which evidence caused the change}
- Approvers and date: ${28:Names or governance group; YYYY-MM-DD}
- References: ${29:Evaluations, standards, RFCs, ADRs, incidents, and vendor material}

Discovery.description: Create a lightweight engineering discovery scratchpad
body: # Discovery: ${1:Topic}
<!-- Temporary engineering scratchpad. Delete irrelevant prompts and write rough notes freely. -->
<!-- Promote useful conclusions into durable documents, then discard this document. -->
- Started: ${2:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Intended output: ${3:RFC, PRD, ADR, technical design, architecture overview, data contract, runbook, migration, threat model, postmortem, engineering investment assessment, or radar entry}
## 1. Goal
- What am I trying to understand or decide? ${4:Question or desired outcome}
- Boundaries: ${5:Relevant scope, constraints, and explicit non-goals}
## 2. Questions
- [ ] ${6:Question to answer}
## 3. Technical Reconnaissance
<!-- Keep only the lenses relevant to this discovery. -->
- Software and architecture: ${7:Code paths, components, boundaries, state, interfaces, dependencies, tests, and ownership}
- Data and platform: ${8:Producers, consumers, contracts, schemas, lineage, batch or stream semantics, quality, replay, and lifecycle}
- Operations and delivery: ${9:Workload, SLOs, failures, observability, security, capacity, cost, deployment, and support}
## 4. Notes and Evidence
- ${10:YYYY-MM-DD — source, command, interview, metric, profile, or experiment — note or result — link}
## 5. Assumptions and Unknowns
- Assumption to verify: ${11:Assumption or hypothesis}
- Unknown or blocker: ${12:Missing information and how to obtain it}
## 6. Findings and Candidate Directions
- Finding: ${13:What the evidence currently supports}
- Why it matters: ${14:Effect on requirements, invariants, design, risk, or operations}
- Candidate direction: ${15:Potential approach; not yet an approved decision}
- Evidence and tradeoffs: ${16:Support, drawbacks, risks, and remaining uncertainty}
## 7. Promote to Durable Documentation
- Carry forward: ${17:Confirmed requirements, constraints, invariants, findings, and source links}
- Keep open: ${18:Decisions, risks, assumptions, and unanswered questions}
- Create or update: ${19:Target documents, next actions, and tracking links when work is promoted}
# EIA ${1:EIA-000}: ${2:Title}
<!-- Source of truth: evidence, expected value, cost, risk, recommendation, and decision for a proposed engineering investment. -->
<!-- Delete non-applicable sections before review. Copy list items as needed. -->
- Author: ${3:Name}
- Owner: ${4:Name}
- Reviewers: ${5:Names or roles}
- Created: ${6:YYYY-MM-DD}
- Related PRDs, RFCs, and ADRs: ${7:IDs or links}
- Status: ${8|Draft,Proposed,Accepted,Rejected,Superseded|}
## 1. Executive Summary
- ${9:What is being evaluated, the conclusion, and the expected return on investment}
## 2. Proposed Change
- ${10:Change being evaluated, intended outcome, scope, measurable baseline and target, affected volume, and evaluation horizon}
## 3. Current System
- High-level architecture: ${11:Relevant components, boundaries, and dependencies}
- Current flow: ${12:Relevant data and control flows}
- Data volume and bandwidth: ${13:Current and forecast volumes, rates, and limits}
- Performance constraints: ${14:Hardware or software constraints that impose lower bounds}
- Known bottlenecks: ${15:Observed bottlenecks and supporting evidence}
- Invariants: ${16:Properties the proposed change must preserve}
## 4. Assumptions
- ${17:ASS-001}: ${18:Assumption the assessment depends on}
- Validation: ${19:Evidence required and how it will be obtained}
## 5. Technical Analysis
- Rating rubric: ${20:Definitions for Unassessed, Low, Moderate, and High or policy link}
<!-- Copy the analysis item for each relevant technical dimension; omit irrelevant dimensions. -->
<!-- Options: Compute, Memory, Storage, I/O, Network, Concurrency, Scalability, Reliability, Operability, Security, Maintainability, Cost. -->
- ${21:ANA-001}: [${22|Compute,Memory,Storage,I/O,Network,Concurrency,Scalability,Reliability,Operability,Security,Maintainability,Cost|}]: ${23:Concern or finding}
- Analysis: ${24:How the concern relates to the current system and proposed change}
- Expected impact: ${25:How the proposed change is expected to affect this concern}
<!-- Options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High. -->
- Expected benefit: ${26|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High|}
<!-- Options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High. -->
- Expected risk: ${27|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High|}
## 6. Evidence
<!-- Options: Benchmark, Documentation, Production metric, Experiment, Profile. -->
- ${28:EVI-001} [${29|Benchmark,Documentation,Production metric,Experiment,Profile|}]: ${30:Source or method}
- Finding: ${31:What the evidence demonstrates and its limitations}
## 7. Engineering Cost
<!-- Retain only cost categories that materially affect the investment decision. -->
- Estimate summary: ${32:Basis, confidence, and important exclusions}
- Research: ${33:Number of days or weeks}
- Prototype: ${34:Number of days or weeks}
- Implementation: ${35:Number of days or weeks}
- Refactoring: ${36:Number of days or weeks}
- Testing: ${37:Number of days or weeks}
- Rollout: ${38:Number of days or weeks}
- Ongoing maintenance: ${39:Number of days or weeks per period}
- Financial cost: ${40:One-time labor, vendor, licensing, procurement, and migration expenditure}
- Ongoing operating cost: ${41:Infrastructure, vendor, licensing, support, and recurring cost per period over the evaluation horizon}
## 8. Risk Assessment
- Method and summary: ${42:Rubric used, overall risk, and basis for the rating}
- Technical risk: ${43:Risk arising from team capability or the technical environment}
- Implementation risk: ${44:Likelihood and impact of blockers, invalid assumptions, or defects}
- Operational risk: ${45:Likelihood and impact of disrupting operations}
- Rollback complexity: ${46:Time, difficulty, and prerequisites for safe recovery}
- Maintenance burden: ${47:Ongoing ownership, support, and upgrade costs}
- Opportunity cost: ${48:Work displaced by this investment}
- Residual risk: ${49:Risk remaining after mitigations — owner — review trigger — tracking link}
## 9. Alternatives Considered
- ${50:ALT-001}: ${51:Alternative}
- Analysis: ${52:How the alternative fits the system and intended outcome}
- Expected impact: ${53:How the alternative would affect the system}
<!-- Options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High. -->
- Expected benefit: ${54|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High|}
<!-- Options: Unassessed, Low, Moderate, High. -->
- Expected risk: ${55|Unassessed,Low,Moderate,High|}
## 10. Recommendation
- ${56:Recommendation based on measurable expected return, cost, evidence, alternatives, and risk}
<!-- Conditional: use the scorecard only when calibrated scoring improves the decision; the recommendation must stand independently. -->
- Scoring method: ${57:Weighting, interpretation, decision thresholds, and treatment of TBD scores}
<!-- Score each criterion from 1 (weakens the investment case) to 5 (strengthens it). -->
| Criterion | Score (TBD or 1–5) | Notes |
| Expected improvement | ${58|TBD,1,2,3,4,5|} | ${59:Expected benefit and basis for the score} |
| Engineering effort | ${60|TBD,1,2,3,4,5|} | ${61:Cost and delivery confidence; lower effort scores higher} |
| Technical risk | ${62|TBD,1,2,3,4,5|} | ${63:Risk and mitigation; lower risk scores higher} |
| Operational complexity | ${64|TBD,1,2,3,4,5|} | ${65:Operational burden; lower complexity scores higher} |
| Evidence strength | ${66|TBD,1,2,3,4,5|} | ${67:Quality and relevance of supporting evidence} |
| Confidence | ${68|TBD,1,2,3,4,5|} | ${69:Confidence in the assessment and remaining uncertainty} |
## 11. Decision and Follow-up
- Outcome: ${70|Pending,Proceed,Do not proceed,Deferred|}
- Decision owner and date: ${71:Name or role; YYYY-MM-DD}
- Review trigger: ${72:Date, evidence threshold, delivery milestone, or material assumption change}
- Post-investment validation: ${73:Owner, date, tracking link, evidence source, and comparison of realized outcomes and costs against the forecast}
